acceptance_targets: [CH18_CRAFT_01] Reading the revised material from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:199 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:325, a cold reader can name one new boundary, procedure, or choice Draco and Hermione have made together that was not already handed to them by Narcissa's revelation; if the scene still goes from apology to hand-holding and research without that new proof, fail.

[CH18_DIALOGUE_01] In the revised dialogue around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:93 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:117 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:223 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:229, no speaker delivers a universal abstraction about 'any relationship' or repeats 'the house makes the question visible' verbatim, and at least one beat of understanding lands through interruption, silence, or concrete remembered detail rather than a clean explanatory speech.

[CH18_DIALOGUE_02] At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:201, Draco's first reply is no longer the exact string 'Yeah.' and no style-bible example line appears verbatim anywhere in the revised chapter; the replacement still reads as a terse, emotionally defended acknowledgment rather than a full absolution.

[CH18_PROSE_01] From snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:329 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:343, the revised ending still includes the first-name exchange, the resumed work, and the live external crisis, but it does not contain an abstract thesis paragraph that restates the bond/doubt dynamic after those physical beats have landed; a reader should be able to infer the chosen uncertainty from scene action alone.

[CH18_PROSE_02] Across the revised beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:111, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:161, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:165, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:197, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:243, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:283, neither the exact scaffold 'The house did nothing' nor the sentence-start 'The question was' appears more than once, and every remaining return to those ideas adds a clearly different piece of physical information or changed relationship pressure that a reader can point to on the page.
[SPECIFIC_PARTICULAR_CH18] Pass if chapter 18 contains at most one instance. Fail if more than one remains.

[BOND_RESTATING_CH18] Pass if the section between Narcissa's departure and Hermione's arrival (snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4954-4968) moves through the bond question in under 10 lines rather than re-processing it at length. Fail if Draco's interior monologue restates the uncertainty in the same terms as chapters 11, 15, or 17.
[R-07] Ch18 arrival description no longer uses 'escaping' or synonymous phrasing for Hermione's hair.
[R-11] Ch18 no longer explains that the grey eyes are a Malfoy/Black inheritance trait.
[R-18] Ch18 has no more than 2 Mark-pain references, each tied to new information or a character decision.
[R-41] Ch18 has em-dashes in no more than 13 prose lines (<=8%).
[R-59] Ch18 has no more than 31 She-opened sentences (<=7%).
[R-72] Ch18 has no more than 31 He-opened sentences (<=7%).

findings.acceptance_test: Reading the revised material from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:199 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:325, a cold reader can name one new boundary, procedure, or choice Draco and Hermione have made together that was not already handed to them by Narcissa's revelation; if the scene still goes from apology to hand-holding and research without that new proof, fail.
findings.problem: The reunion turn is under-earned on the page. Hermione apologizes, Draco relays Narcissa's new information, and the scene moves quickly into the drawing room and resumed work, but the chapter never forces the two of them to make one new practical trust choice together after the information asymmetry that broke them. As written, the return lands as permission and atmosphere rather than as a fresh relational proof.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the exchange starting at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:199 and carrying through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:230 so the reunion produces a concrete new term of engagement that Narcissa's earlier explanation did not already supply: a rule about disclosure, a test for reopening the archive, a condition for staying, or an explicit cost they are both accepting. Keep the drawing-room movement at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:245 and the library resumption at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:323, but make those beats consequences of that on-page choice rather than substitutes for it.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised dialogue around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:93 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:117 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:223 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:229, no speaker delivers a universal abstraction about 'any relationship' or repeats 'the house makes the question visible' verbatim, and at least one beat of understanding lands through interruption, silence, or concrete remembered detail rather than a clean explanatory speech.
findings.problem: The chapter's key conversations slip into composed thesis-speech. Narcissa's reveal arrives as a polished mini-lecture with a ready-made greenhouse analogy and a universal claim about 'any relationship,' then Draco repeats the same clean thematic language to Hermione almost verbatim. That creates aphoristic, book-summary dialogue where pressured scene speech should sound more partial, costly, and character-bound.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:93, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:101, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:107, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:117, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:223, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:229, break the exposition into more scene-pressured language. Keep Narcissa formal, but let the thought arrive through family-specific detail, visible effort, interruption, or omission rather than polished universal doctrine; let Draco and Hermione answer the immediate consequences instead of re-quoting the chapter thesis to each other.

findings.acceptance_test: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:201, Draco's first reply is no longer the exact string 'Yeah.' and no style-bible example line appears verbatim anywhere in the revised chapter; the replacement still reads as a terse, emotionally defended acknowledgment rather than a full absolution.
findings.problem: The chapter reproduces a style-bible calibration line verbatim: Draco's 'Yeah.' matches an example line from the voice profiles exactly. The review rules explicitly forbid carrying example lines into manuscript text, and here the leakage is visible because the beat is doing almost no character-specific work beyond placeholder acknowledgment.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the reply at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:201 with a Draco answer that remains brief and guarded but is not a verbatim example-line carryover. The surrounding apology beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:199 should still feel clipped and unreconciled, just written in his own local language.

findings.acceptance_test: From snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:329 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:343, the revised ending still includes the first-name exchange, the resumed work, and the live external crisis, but it does not contain an abstract thesis paragraph that restates the bond/doubt dynamic after those physical beats have landed; a reader should be able to infer the chosen uncertainty from scene action alone.
findings.problem: The ending keeps translating meaning after the chapter has already arrived. Draco saying her name, Hermione answering with his, the fire catching, and the two of them returning to the texts are sufficient concrete closure beats; the abstract paragraph at line 341 paraphrases the chapter's thesis for the reader instead of trusting those embodied actions to carry it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or replace the explanatory summary centered on snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:341. Let the first-name shift at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:329, the domestic return at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:337, and the shared research image at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:343 carry the ending. If any narration remains in that slot, it needs to introduce new pressure or cost, not restate what the scene has already demonstrated.

findings.acceptance_test: Across the revised beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:111, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:161, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:165, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:197, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:243, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:283, neither the exact scaffold 'The house did nothing' nor the sentence-start 'The question was' appears more than once, and every remaining return to those ideas adds a clearly different piece of physical information or changed relationship pressure that a reader can point to on the page.
findings.problem: Two marked sentence scaffolds recur mechanically enough to read as autopilot rather than motif: 'The question was...' returns in nearly the same thematic function, and 'The house did nothing' appears three times. Because each reuse serves another null-state reminder rather than a materially new angle, the repetitions flatten escalation in a chapter that otherwise depends on fine tonal modulation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the repeated scaffolds at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:111, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:161, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:165, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:197, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:243, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:283. Keep the motifs of unresolved doubt and house-inertia, but vary the diction and give each recurrence a new physical or relational payload instead of another near-identical declarative reset.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 18 contains at most one instance. Fail if more than one remains.

evidence.excerpt: 4956: He sat in the cold library with what she had given him. The specific grief of a woman telling her son that her marriage — real, chosen, survived — had never moved the house the way his messy, broken, possibly-ended thing had moved it. She'd carried that knowledge for years. She'd carried it alone, and today she'd set it down in front of him, and the generosity of that was the kind of thing he didn't have language for.

4986: She didn't ask to come in. She stood on the step and he stood in the doorway and the gap between them was three feet of cold air and six weeks of proximity and a week of absence and the specific damage of his last words to her, which had been precise and cruel, and her silence before walking out, which had been its own precision.

5086: She was still holding his hand. He could feel the calluses on her fingers from years of wand-work, the specific architecture of her grip. The Mark under the gauze had eased. Not gone quiet — it was far from quiet — but the grinding had softened to something he could carry, the way a noise you've been straining against loses its edge when another sound enters the room.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 4986: She didn't ask to come in. She stood on the step and he stood in the doorway and the gap between them was three feet of cold air and six weeks of proximity and a week of absence and the specific damage of his last words to her, which had been precise and cruel, and her silence before walking out, which had been its own precision.

findings.problem: Chapter 18 — the novel's final chapter — uses 'the specific grief of a woman telling her son' (already cited in context), 'the specific damage of his last words to her' (4986), and 'the specific architecture of her grip' (5086). The final chapter should consolidate the novel's voice at its most distinctive; these formula instances make it sound like the rest of the book.
findings.rewrite_direction: REPLACE instances at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4986 and 5086. KEEP one if it earns its place — line 4956 can stay if it's the sole instance. At line 5086: 'the specific architecture of her grip' — 'architecture' is doing real work here given the novel's theme; restructure to keep the metaphor without the formula wrapper.

acceptance_test.excerpt:
4954: He heard her footsteps recede down the entrance hall, measured and unhurried. The front door opened and closed. The house did not adjust — no corridor shifting, no temperature change. The house did nothing at all.
4955: 
4956: He sat in the cold library with what she had given him. The specific grief of a woman telling her son that her marriage — real, chosen, survived — had never moved the house the way his messy, broken, possibly-ended thing had moved it. She'd carried that knowledge for years. She'd carried it alone, and today she'd set it down in front of him, and the generosity of that was the kind of thing he didn't have language for.
4957: 
4958: The Mark ground against the bone. He pressed his wrapped forearm to the chair and closed his eyes and sat with the question, which was the same question it had always been but shaped differently now. Not: was this fabricated or real? The house couldn't fabricate. His mother had said so, and his mother did not say things she had not verified. The question was: how much of what he felt was his own and how much was the house's amplification? And the answer she'd given him, which was not an answer at all, was that this was the que...

evidence.excerpt: 4902: He was quiet for a long time. The information reorganized itself in his head with the slow heaviness of furniture being moved in a room too small for it. He thought of her hands on his forearm, the Mark going still under her diagnostic touch. He thought of her in the library at midnight, pushing hair off her face, mouthing a translation. He thought of the kiss — clumsy, terrified, the best thing that had happened to him in years — and he thought of it now with the knowledge that the wanting underneath it had been his. The house had turned up the volume. But the sound had been there.
5016: "That doesn't resolve it entirely," she said. "Amplification is still influence. We still can't know—"

5024: She was quiet. The entrance hall was cold around them. His arm throbbed. She was standing four feet away in her heavy coat with her hair loose and her work bag over her shoulder, and he wanted to touch her with a clarity that was either entirely his own or partly the house's or both, and the distinction mattered and also it didn't, and he was exhausted by the distinction mattering.

findings.problem: Chapter 18 receives Narcissa's revelation that the house 'cannot fabricate' (line 4890), which should materially change the question's terms. Yet the processing at lines 4957-4958 ('the same question it had always been but shaped differently now') and the dialogue at lines 5016-5022 restate the uncertainty again. The question is now resolved on one axis (the seed was real) but the chapter continues processing the amplification-uncertainty axis in the same interior register as previous chapters rather than moving to genuinely new ground.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4950-5025, let Narcissa's revelation do more work. After the 'cannot fabricate' beat, the characters should arrive at changed ground rather than restating the uncertainty. The dialogue at lines 5016-5022 ('I can't separate it... what I feel from what the house did') is effective because it's spoken rather than thought — preserve this but cut the preceding interior-monologue restatement at lines 4957-4958 that dilutes Narcissa's revelation before Draco can act on it.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel is dark, intense, and emotionally devastating. The premise asks for a love story built inside trauma, in a house that may be manipulating its inhabitants, between two people marked by the same war in different ways. The creative risk is not in the darkness — it is in the tenderness. The hardest thing this novel must do is make the quiet moments as fully rendered as the painful ones, and make the desire as specific as the grief. Do not retreat from the body: the Mark examinations are physical events with physical consequences. The kiss is a physical event. The scar is a physical object on a real arm. Do not retreat from the doubt: the Manor's possible manipulation is not a problem to be solved in act three but a condition to be lived inside for the entire novel. Do not retreat from the ugliness of the drawing room scene or the specificity of what Bellatrix did. And do not retreat from the gentleness of the domestic scenes — tenderness between damaged people is harder to write than pain and should be given equal craft attention.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure equivalent to bad prose. When the scene calls for specificity — in pain, in desire, in shame, in violence, in grief — render it. The drawing room scene must feel like being in the room. The Mark examinations must feel like touch. The scar reveal must feel like exposure. Fading to implication at the moment of consequence wastes every scene that built to that moment.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Narcissa. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or Narcissa's default register. An unjustified uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be flagged and revised.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or domestic ones. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters are allowed for compression; three-scene chapters should not all follow the same internal rhythm. Vary dialogue density — some chapters are conversation-heavy, others are predominantly interior. Never give two consecutive chapters the same structural shape. A chapter that builds to a confrontation should not be followed by another chapter that builds to a confrontation; a quiet aftermath chapter should not be followed by another quiet aftermath chapter. The rhythm of the book as a whole should feel like breathing — expansion and contraction, tension and release, pressure and air.
prose_style_profile.diction: Literary but not ornate. The prose should feel crafted without feeling written — precise where precision matters, rough where roughness is true. Avoid both the polished-prestige-novel voice and the genre-fiction utility voice. The diction should sound like someone telling a story who is very good at telling stories but not performing the act of telling.
forbidden_drift_patterns: The metaphor chain — a comparison that extends across three or more sentences and becomes the scene's organizing image rather than a local effect
Emotional stage directions — 'she felt a surge of' or 'something tightened in his chest' or 'a cold knot formed' as substitutes for specific rendered emotion
The retrospective frame — 'she would later realize' or 'he didn't know then' or other future-looking narrative commentary that drains present-tense urgency
Symmetrical sentence pairs — 'She was X. He was Y.' as a repeated structural pattern, especially in dual-POV transitions
The ambient-magic-as-mood fallback — using the Manor's temperature or the Mark's ache to externalize emotional states that should be rendered through behavior and thought
Catalogue of sensory impressions used as scene-setting rather than scene-building — lists of what a room looks, smells, sounds like that delay rather than create dramatic pressure
The literary-fiction semicolon habit — using semicolons to connect observations that would land harder as separate sentences or would reveal more if left unconnected
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, alternating by chapter. Hermione's chapters are in her interiority — analytical, self-aware, faster. Draco's chapters are in his — more guarded, more sensory, the observations sharper and the self-knowledge harder-won. The POV character's biases should be legible without being announced.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable. Default to medium-length sentences with occasional long, accumulating ones and occasional short declaratives for force. Avoid sustained staccato. Avoid sustained flowing prose. The rhythm should shift with scene pressure — tighter under tension, more expansive in quiet and reflective passages.
sensory_bias: tactile — the Mark examinations, the Manor's surfaces, temperature, skin contact, the physical experience of proximity
architectural and spatial — the Manor as sensory environment, corridors, rooms, light, the way space itself communicates
proprioceptive — body awareness, the resonance between them as a felt physical phenomenon, pain and pleasure as data
